description: JSON encoder and decoder for Python objects. Provides serialization of Python dicts, lists, strings, and numbers to JSON format and deserialization back.

functions.name: loads
functions.signature: loads(s, encoding=None, cls=None, object_hook=None, parse_float=None, parse_int=None, parse_constant=None, object_pairs_hook=None)

params.name: s
params.description: A JSON string to deserialize

params.name: encoding
params.description: The encoding used to interpret the string (default UTF-8). Ignored in Python 2.7 when s is unicode.

params.name: cls
params.description: Custom JSONDecoder subclass to use for deserialization

params.name: object_hook
params.description: Function called with the result of every JSON object decoded, allowing custom transformations

params.name: parse_float
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON float to be decoded (default float)

params.name: parse_int
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON int to be decoded (default int)

params.name: parse_constant
params.description: Function called with the string of JSON constants (-Infinity, Infinity, NaN)

params.name: object_pairs_hook
params.description: Function called with an ordered list of pairs for every JSON object, allowing ordered dict construction

returns.description: Python object (dict, list, str, unicode, int, float, bool, or None)

functions.description: Deserialize a JSON string to a Python object.

functions.name: dumps
functions.signature: dumps(obj, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=True, check_circular=True, allow_nan=True, cls=None, indent=None, separators=None, encoding='utf-8', default=None, sort_keys=False)

params.name: obj
params.description: The Python object to serialize to JSON

params.name: skipkeys
params.description: If True, dict keys that are not basic types (str, unicode, int, long, float, bool, None) will be skipped instead of raising TypeError

params.name: ensure_ascii
params.description: If True, all non-ASCII characters in the output are escaped with \uXXXX sequences

params.name: check_circular
params.description: If False, the circular reference check for container types is skipped (may cause OverflowError or worse)

params.name: allow_nan
params.description: If False, serialization of float values that are out of range (nan, inf, -inf) will raise ValueError

params.name: cls
params.description: Custom JSONEncoder subclass to use for serialization

params.name: indent
params.description: Number of spaces for pretty-printing indentation. None produces the most compact representation.

params.name: separators
params.description: Tuple of (item_separator, key_separator). Default is (', ', ': ') with indent or (',', ': ') without.

params.name: encoding
params.description: Character encoding for str instances (default 'utf-8')

params.name: default
params.description: Function called for objects that are not serializable. Should return a serializable object or raise TypeError.

params.name: sort_keys
params.description: If True, the output of dictionaries will be sorted by key

functions.description: Serialize a Python object to a JSON-formatted string.

functions.name: load
functions.signature: load(fp, encoding=None, cls=None, object_hook=None, parse_float=None, parse_int=None, parse_constant=None, object_pairs_hook=None)

params.name: fp
params.description: A file-like object with a .read() method containing a JSON document

params.name: encoding
params.description: The encoding used to interpret the file contents

params.name: cls
params.description: Custom JSONDecoder subclass to use for deserialization

params.name: object_hook
params.description: Function called with the result of every JSON object decoded

params.name: parse_float
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON float to be decoded

params.name: parse_int
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON int to be decoded

params.name: parse_constant
params.description: Function called with the string of JSON constants (-Infinity, Infinity, NaN)

params.name: object_pairs_hook
params.description: Function called with an ordered list of pairs for every JSON object

returns.description: Python object (dict, list, str, unicode, int, float, bool, or None)

functions.description: Deserialize a JSON document read from a file-like object to a Python object.

functions.name: dump
functions.signature: dump(obj, fp, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=True, check_circular=True, allow_nan=True, cls=None, indent=None, separators=None, encoding='utf-8', default=None, sort_keys=False)

params.name: obj
params.description: The Python object to serialize to JSON

params.name: fp
params.description: A file-like object with a .write() method

params.name: skipkeys
params.description: If True, dict keys that are not basic types will be skipped instead of raising TypeError

params.name: ensure_ascii
params.description: If True, all non-ASCII characters in the output are escaped with \uXXXX sequences

params.name: check_circular
params.description: If False, the circular reference check for container types is skipped

params.name: allow_nan
params.description: If False, serialization of out-of-range float values will raise ValueError

params.name: cls
params.description: Custom JSONEncoder subclass to use for serialization

params.name: indent
params.description: Number of spaces for pretty-printing indentation

params.name: separators
params.description: Tuple of (item_separator, key_separator)

params.name: encoding
params.description: Character encoding for str instances (default 'utf-8')

params.name: default
params.description: Function called for objects that are not serializable

params.name: sort_keys
params.description: If True, the output of dictionaries will be sorted by key

returns.description: Writes JSON to the file-like object; returns None

functions.description: Serialize a Python object as a JSON-formatted stream to a file-like object.

classes.name: JSONEncoder
classes.description: Extensible JSON encoder for Python data structures. Supports str, unicode, int, long, float, bool, None, list, tuple, and dict by default.

constructors.signature: JSONEncoder(skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=True, check_circular=True, allow_nan=True, sort_keys=False, indent=None, separators=None, encoding='utf-8', default=None)

params.name: skipkeys
params.description: If True, keys that are not basic types will be skipped

params.name: ensure_ascii
params.description: If True, escape all non-ASCII characters in output

params.name: check_circular
params.description: If True (default), check for circular references during encoding

params.name: allow_nan
params.description: If True (default), allow NaN, Infinity, and -Infinity as values (not valid JSON but accepted by JavaScript)

params.name: sort_keys
params.description: If True, sort dictionary output by key

params.name: indent
params.description: Number of spaces for pretty-printing indentation

params.name: separators
params.description: Tuple of (item_separator, key_separator)

params.name: encoding
params.description: Character encoding for str instances

params.name: default
params.description: Function for objects that cannot be serialized

constructors.description: Create a new JSONEncoder instance with the given options.

methods.name: encode

params.name: o
params.description: Python object to serialize

methods.description: Return a JSON string representation of a Python data structure.

methods.name: default

params.name: o
params.description: Object that is not serializable by default

returns.description: A serializable object, or raises TypeError

methods.description: Override this method in a subclass to serialize additional types. Called for objects that cannot otherwise be serialized. Should return a serializable object or raise TypeError.

methods.name: iterencode

params.name: o
params.description: Python object to serialize

returns.description: Generator yielding string chunks of the JSON encoding

methods.description: Encode the given object and yield each string representation as available. Useful for encoding large objects incrementally.

classes.name: JSONDecoder
classes.description: Simple JSON decoder. Performs the following translations by default: JSON object to dict, array to list, string to unicode, number to int/float, true/false to True/False, null to None.

constructors.signature: JSONDecoder(encoding=None, object_hook=None, parse_float=None, parse_int=None, parse_constant=None, strict=True, object_pairs_hook=None)

params.name: encoding
params.description: Encoding used to decode the input string

params.name: object_hook
params.description: Function called with the result of every JSON object decoded

params.name: parse_float
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON float

params.name: parse_int
params.description: Function called with the string of every JSON int

params.name: parse_constant
params.description: Function called with the string of JSON constants

params.name: strict
params.description: If False, allow control characters inside strings

params.name: object_pairs_hook
params.description: Function called with an ordered list of pairs for every JSON object

methods.name: decode

params.name: s
params.description: A JSON string to deserialize

returns.description: Python representation of the JSON document

methods.description: Return the Python representation of a JSON string. Raises ValueError if the string is not valid JSON.

methods.name: raw_decode

params.name: s
params.description: A JSON string to deserialize

params.name: idx
params.description: Index in s where the JSON document begins (default 0)

returns.description: A 2-tuple of (Python object, index where the document ended)

methods.description: Decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end. Returns a tuple of the decoded object and the index to the end of the document.
